title: Rosa and the Hungry Baby Birds
synopsis: Rosa discovers baby birds that have fallen from their nest and feels scared about how to help them. With her friend Marcus, she learns to be brave and finds a creative solution to feed the hungry babies until their mama returns.

pages.text: Rosa was playing in her backyard when she heard tiny chirping sounds. She looked down and saw two baby birds on the ground. They looked scared and hungry.

illustration.description: Rosa, a young Latina girl with curly dark hair in pigtails, wearing a bright yellow sundress, crouches down in a green backyard. Two small brown baby birds sit on the grass looking up at her with big eyes. The scene has warm, sunny lighting with construction paper texture.
illustration.alt: Rosa discovering baby birds in her backyard

pages.text: Rosa felt scared too. She didn't know how to help the baby birds. 'What should I do?' she whispered softly.

illustration.description: Close-up of Rosa's face showing a worried expression, her brown eyes wide with concern. Her hands are pressed to her cheeks. The baby birds are visible in the bottom corner, still chirping. Soft blue sky background with textured clouds.
illustration.alt: Rosa looking worried about the baby birds

pages.text: Rosa's friend Marcus came over. He was a kind boy who loved animals. When he saw the baby birds, he felt sad for them too.

illustration.description: Marcus, a Black boy with short curly hair wearing a bright red t-shirt and blue shorts, walks into the yard. Rosa points to the baby birds. Both children have concerned expressions. The backyard has colorful flowers and green grass with textured appearance.
illustration.alt: Marcus arriving to help Rosa with the baby birds

pages.text: 'We can help them!' Marcus said. 'My mom taught me that baby birds need food and water.' Rosa started to feel a little brave.

illustration.description: Marcus kneels next to Rosa, both children looking at the baby birds. Marcus has a gentle, encouraging smile while Rosa's expression shows hope mixed with nervousness. The baby birds are between them, and there are dandelions scattered in the grass.
illustration.alt: Marcus encouraging Rosa to help the baby birds

pages.text: Rosa and Marcus brought water in a small cap and found some worms in the garden. They fed the hungry babies very gently.

illustration.description: Rosa and Marcus sitting cross-legged on the grass, carefully feeding the baby birds with a bottle cap of water and small worms. Both children have focused, caring expressions. Bright green grass surrounds them with construction paper texture and warm sunlight.
illustration.alt: Rosa and Marcus feeding the baby birds

pages.text: Soon, mama bird came back! She was so happy to see her babies safe. Rosa felt proud and brave. She had helped her new feathered friends!

illustration.description: A large brown mama bird flies down to her babies while Rosa and Marcus watch with big happy smiles and raised arms in celebration. The baby birds chirp excitedly. Bright blue sky with fluffy white clouds and green trees in the background, all with cheerful construction paper style.
illustration.alt: Mama bird returning to her babies while Rosa and Marcus celebrate

metadata.illustrationStyle: Bright, cheerful cartoon style with expressive faces. Bold primary colors. Rounded, friendly shapes. Slightly textured backgrounds like construction paper.

questions.question: How did Rosa feel when she first saw the baby birds?
questions.explanation: Rosa felt scared because she didn't know how to help the baby birds.

questions.question: Who came to help Rosa?
questions.explanation: Marcus, Rosa's friend who loves animals, came to help her.

questions.question: What did Rosa and Marcus give the baby birds?
questions.explanation: They gave the baby birds water in a bottle cap and worms from the garden.

questions.question: How did Rosa feel at the end of the story?
questions.explanation: Rosa felt proud and brave because she helped the baby birds.

Marcus said 'We can help them!' Rosa started to feel a little _____. They brought _____ and worms to feed the babies.
